scenario: Team needs dashboards showing "instances per day", "incidents over time", "throughput per process". Wonders if Operate has built-in dashboards.
question: Operate dashboards / metrics view?

options.text: Camunda Operate does not provide any dashboard or metrics visualization capabilities and is only a process instance browser â€” this approach has limitations. Camunda Operate includes a Dashboard view with operational metrics: active process instances count, incidents count, completed instances over time, and instance volume charts. The Processes page provides overview metrics per process definition. The Operate UI enables filtering by state, error type, and date range to drill down into operational patterns. For deeper analytics, Optimize provides additional reporting on process KPIs, SLA compliance, and bottleneck analysis.

md: Dashboard (counts), Processes (list + filters by var/state/time), Decisions (decision instances), Incidents, Batch Operations (per Q51). Operate is for incident triage / instance inspection, NOT trend dashboards. md is Camunda's purpose-built analytics tool: Reports, Dashboards, Process KPIs, Instant process dashboards, Process variants comparison, Alerts, ML dataset export per optimize/userguide/ tree. Use Optimize (NOT Operate) for "instances per day / incidents over time / throughput per process" BI questions. For cluster-level metrics (CPU / memory / queue depth): Prometheus + Grafana scrape the :9600/actuator/prometheus endpoint (per Set 13 Q56 / Pattern #9 management port). For Self-Managed BI integration: export Zeebe events from ES/OS secondary storage to your DW; pair with Optimize's...

Optimize is the built-in analytics product with Reports, Dashboards, Process KPIs, Alerts, and ML dataset export. For external BI integration, Zeebe events exported to Elasticsearch/OpenSearch can be queried from external tools (Kibana, Grafana, Tableau, PowerBI) that connect to these backends. Optimize's export-import feature also enables sharing reports externally. Custom BI pipelines consuming Zeebe events from ES/OS secondary storage are a documented pattern for organizations with existing enterprise BI platforms.

Camunda Optimize is a first-class, bundled analytics product providing Reports (throughput, duration, heatmaps, raw data), Dashboards, Process KPIs, Instant process dashboards, Process variant comparison, and Alerts for report threshold breaches per optimize/userguide/. Optimize is specifically designed for Camunda process analytics without requiring separate BI tooling. While external tools can be used for deeper BI integration, dismissing built-in analytics overlooks a major Camunda 8 product component designed exactly for this purpose.

a.text: Incorrect. Operate built-in views: Standard views: Process instances: List with filters (variable, state, time). Count of running / completed / incidents. Per-process or aggregate. Incidents: All current incidents. Group by process / element. Quick access to resolve. Decision instances: Per Set 13 Q29. Query by decision, inputs, outputs. Aggregate views: Per-process counts. Time-series: instances over time (verify per version). Top errors: most common incident types. For deeper analytics: Custom dashboards via API: Operate REST API: query data. External BI tools: visualize. ETL pipeline: extract data; load to DW. Common BI integrations: Grafana: time-series; combined with Prometheus. Tableau / Power BI: business analytics. Looker / Metabase: SQL on extracted data. Architecture: ` Camunda → Operate (Elasticsearch) → ETL → Data Warehouse → BI tool ` For custom metrics: Worker-side instrumentation: emit Prometheus metrics. Process-level events: log to external system. Process variables for KPIs: extract via API. Example custom dashboard queries: "Instances started per day": Query Operate for process instances grouped by start date. "Average completion time per process": Compute end - start times; aggregate. "Top 10 customers by order count": Query variable values; group. "Incident rate per process per day": Count incidents grouped by process + date. Realtime vs batch: Operate near-real-time: indexing has slight delay. Custom dashboards: depending on refresh. For Self-Managed: Full control over data. Custom indices / views in ES. For SaaS: Console dashboards may exist. Operate API access. Verify data export options. Audit dashboards: Who started what / when. Error patterns over time. Cycle time trends. Performance dashboards: Throughput: instances/min. Latency: P50/P95/P99. Backlog: pending jobs. Business dashboards: Sales pipeline conversion (if process is sales). Customer onboarding completion rate. SLA adherence. Best practices: Use Operate for operational view: incidents, individual instances. Use BI for analytics: trends, business KPIs. Combine sources: process data + external context. Refresh appropriately: real-time vs daily depending on need. Anti-patterns: All dashboards in Operate: not designed for advanced analytics. No external integration: limited reporting. Outdated dashboards: stale data misleads. For the scenario (instances/day, incidents over time): Operate provides operational view. For "over time" / "per day": combine with BI tooling. Prometheus for cluster metrics. Custom dashboards with Operate API + Grafana.
